title: Quarantine Release Audit
spl:
index=nac (sourcetype="nac:quarantine" OR sourcetype="cisco:ise:admin")
| search "quarantine" AND (released OR "cleared" OR "unquarantine")
| table _time, admin_user, endpoint_mac, action, ticket_id
| sort -_time
description: Audit trail of who released endpoints from quarantine and whether release matched policy (e.g., IT-only, ticket required).
value: Audit trail of who released endpoints from quarantine and whether release matched policy (e.g., IT-only, ticket required).
implementation: Map `ticket_id` from workflow; alert when `isnull(ticket_id)` and action is manual release. Join ServiceNow for approved changes.
visualization: Table (release audit), Timeline (releases), Bar chart (releases by admin).
knownFalsePositives: Quarantines from BYOD enrollment, devices with stale certs, or compliance check failures during patch cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk_TA_cisco-ise, `nac:quarantine`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Admin audit + quarantine logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map `ticket_id` from workflow; alert when `isnull(ticket_id)` and action is manual release. Join ServiceNow for approved changes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac (sourcetype="nac:quarantine" OR sourcetype="cisco:ise:admin")
| search "quarantine" AND (released OR "cleared" OR "unquarantine")
| table _time, admin_user, endpoint_mac, action, ticket_id
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Quarantine Release Audit** — Audit trail of who released endpoints from quarantine and whether release matched policy (e.g., IT-only, ticket required).

Documented **Data sources**: Admin audit + quarantine logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_cisco-ise, `nac:quarantine`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: nac:quarantine, cisco:ise:admin. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="nac:quarantine". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Quarantine Release Audit**): table _time, admin_user, endpoint_mac, action, ticket_id
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize

Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (release audit), Timeline (releases), Bar chart (releases by admin).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Email.Email
  by Email.user, Email.action, Email.src, Email.dest span=1h
| sort - count
